cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the text carefully to find out the correct answer. From the text: - The Dutch honored this kindly saint with a yearly festival on December sixth. The Dutch spoke the name "Saint Nikolas" very fast. It sounded like "sinterklaas". When the English said this word, it sounded like "Santa Claus". This means "Santa Claus" was a mishearing or misunderstanding of "Sinterklaas", which came from the Dutch pronunciation of "Saint Nikolas". So option (B) seems correct. The text says the Dutch had a festival on December 6th, not December 25th, so (A) is incorrect. The text doesn't say the Dutch created Santa Claus. It says that the name Santa Claus and Saint Nicholas both come from the Dutch, but doesn't say they created the figure of Santa Claus. So (C) is incorrect. The text says "It is easy to find them by the long lines of children waiting to tell Santa what they want for Christmas."
